(which is 6) possible permutations: 1. Grey puzzle, gold pencil, yellow teddy bear 2. Grey puzzle, yellow teddy bear, gold pencil 3. Gold pencil, grey puzzle, yellow teddy bear 4. Gold pencil, yellow teddy bear, grey puzzle 5. Yellow teddy bear, grey puzzle, gold pencil 6. Yellow teddy bear, gold pencil, grey puzzle Now, the gold object is the gold pencil. So I need to find in which of these permutations the gold pencil is directly followed by another object, and then determine the color of that object. Looking at the six permutations: 1. In permutation 1: Gold pencil is in the middle, so the object to its right is the yellow teddy bear. The color would be yellow. That's option C. 2. In permutation 2: Gold pencil is at the end, so there's no object to its right. 3. In permutation 3: Gold pencil is first, so object to its right is the grey puzzle. Color is grey. That's option P. 4. In permutation 4: Gold pencil is first, object to its right is yellow teddy bear. Color is yellow. Option C. 5. In permutation 5: Gold pencil is last, no object to its right. 6. In permutation 6: Gold pencil is in the middle, object to its right is grey puzzle. Color is grey. Option P. So depending on the arrangement, the color could be either yellow (C) or grey (P). However, the problem doesn't give any additional information about the order of the objects. That would eliminate permutations 2 and 5 where the gold object is at the end. So we're left with permutations 1, 3, 4, and 6. In permutations 1 and 4, the object to the right of the gold object is yellow. In permutations 3 and 6, it's grey. Still two possibilities. So unless there's some implicit information I'm missing.
